search_queries_used: site:nice.org.uk CG109 transient loss consciousness recommendations syncope
rejection_reasons: Patient pages are not clinician guidance.
Epilepsy-only guidance is not an exact syncope source.
population_applicability: Partial: exact for people aged 16 years and over with transient loss of consciousness; the legacy workflow has no explicit age restriction.
setting_applicability: Direct for initial and specialist assessment and follow-up; emergency treatment remains clinician-led.
UAE_applicability: International NICE guidance requires UAE clinician review and local driving, referral, and emergency pathways.
recency_verification: The current official CG109 recommendations, last updated 2023-11-21, were rechecked on 2026-07-13.
superseded_check: CG109 remains the current official NICE transient-loss-of-consciousness guideline.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped event, prodrome, position, duration, witness, associated symptom, focal-neurological, vital-sign, cardiovascular, neurological, preset, and history fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports event and witness history, posture, prodrome, appearance, movements, tongue biting, injury, duration, confusion, focal weakness, previous events, medical and family history, medicines, vital signs, lying and standing blood pressure, cardiovascular examination, and neurological signs.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped ECG, glucose, and haemoglobin or blood-count fields when clinically indicated.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician-performed or reviewed 12-lead ECG and selected additional tests such as glucose or haemoglobin when indicated by the assessment.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped clinician-decided referral and escalation context.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports urgent cardiovascular assessment for ECG abnormality, heart failure, transient loss of consciousness during exertion, family history of sudden cardiac death, new or unexplained breathlessness, or a heart murmur.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped postural blood pressure, ambulatory ECG, and echocardiography review.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician-led orthostatic assessment, repeat or previous ECG review, ambulatory ECG selection, further investigation, and specialist cardiovascular assessment.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped safety, driving, recurrence, and arranged-follow-up documentation.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports documented discussion of the event, tests, driving, safety, and what to do if another event occurs.

unresolved_source_gaps: People younger than 16 years are outside the exact source.
Chest-pain negatives, oxygen saturation, hydration, electrolytes, troponin, generic clinician plans, and medication plans remain unsupported.
Clinical interpretation, driving eligibility, emergency disposition, and qualified clinician review remain clinician responsibilities.
